title: User-ID Mapping Identity Source and Authentication Log Triage
description: Trace identity mappings from User-ID agents and external sources into the firewall cache, verify mapping provenance, and inspect authd evidence.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook inspects how Palo Alto learns and uses identity with #show user user-id-agent state all, #show user group-mapping statistics, #show log userid, and #less mp-log authd.log.

  1. Verify ingestion: confirm that User-ID agents and server-monitor paths are active.
  2. Confirm mapping state: inspect group and IP-to-user mapping caches.
  3. Prove origin: tie mappings back to sources such as Kerberos, GlobalProtect, or vpn-client style inputs.
  4. Correct stale state: review authd evidence and clear cache entries if needed.
metadata.commandsBlock:
# Step 1: Verify User-ID ingestion health
> show user user-id-agent state all
> show user server-monitor state all
> show user server-monitor statistics

# Step 2: Inspect group and IP-user mapping state
> show user group-mapping statistics
> show user group-mapping state all
> show user ip-user-mapping all
> show user ip-user-mapping ip <ip-address>

# Step 3: Attribute mappings to a source
> show log userid datasourcetype equal kerberos
> show log userid datasourcetype equal globalprotect
> show log userid datasource equal vpn-client

# Step 4: Review logs and clear stale entries
> less mp-log authd.log
clear user-cache all
clear user-cache ip <ip-address/netmask>
prerequisites: A user, group, IP address, or identity source worth investigating
attackerGains: Visibility into identity-source health and provenance
detectionNotes: Identity queries are operationally quiet and should be controlled by management-plane access policy
Unexpected User-ID and authd inspection can expose sensitive user-context data

steps.name: Query User-ID Ingestion Health
steps.tooltip: This is the first decision point before assuming a policy problem is actually a mapping problem.
steps.description: The operator checks User-ID agent and server-monitor health to confirm that identity data is being ingested at all.
steps.cli:
> show user user-id-agent state all
> show user server-monitor state all
> show user server-monitor statistics

steps.name: Populate and Maintain Mapping Cache
steps.tooltip: The cache is only meaningful if upstream sources are actually contributing user mappings.
steps.description: Identity events from sources such as Kerberos feed the firewall's IP-to-user mapping cache.
steps.cli: > show log userid datasourcetype equal kerberos

steps.name: Record Additional VPN-Linked Identity Sources
steps.tooltip: VPN identity sources can create or refresh mappings independently of directory ingestion.
steps.description: GlobalProtect-linked mappings also feed the same cache and can explain user-context differences from directory-only views.
steps.cli: > show log userid datasourcetype equal globalprotect

steps.name: Track VPN Client Mapping Origin
steps.tooltip: This helps prove which source path is actually populating the firewall's user-context state.
steps.description: vpn-client style mapping origin is inspected to determine whether remote-access sources are contributing user context.
steps.cli: > show log userid datasource equal vpn-client

steps.name: Resolve Groups and User Context
steps.tooltip: User mappings and group mappings must both be healthy for identity-based policy to behave correctly.
steps.description: The firewall correlates user mappings with group-mapping state so identity-aware policy can evaluate users and groups correctly.
steps.cli:
> show user group-mapping statistics
> show user group-mapping state all
> show user ip-user-mapping all
> show user ip-user-mapping ip <ip-address>

steps.name: Collect Triage Evidence
steps.tooltip: This step captures the firewall's current belief about users, groups, and source provenance.
steps.description: The operator pulls current mapping and group state into a troubleshooting report for the incident or change review.

steps.name: Inspect authd and Clear Stale Cache
steps.tooltip: This is the corrective step for stale or clearly incorrect identity state.
steps.description: The operator inspects authd evidence and, if required, clears stale mapping state from the cache.
steps.cli:
> less mp-log authd.log
clear user-cache all
clear user-cache ip <ip-address/netmask>
